summary: Cathy O'Neil announces her upcoming participation in Yale's Rebellious Lawyering Conference, an annual student-run public interest gathering focused on progressive legal approaches to social change. She will be on a panel called 'Using Law To Occupy Wall Street' alongside Akshat Tewary from Occupy the SEC and Rebecca Wilkins. The conference is grounded in Gerald Lopez's vision of challenging hierarchies of race, wealth, gender, and expertise within legal practice. O'Neil expresses enthusiasm about the event and her fellow panelists.
key_points: RebLaw is the nation's largest student-run public interest legal conference
The conference focuses on progressive approaches to law and social change, challenging traditional hierarchies
O'Neil's panel connects legal activism to the Occupy Wall Street movement
The event brings together practitioners, students, and community activists
money_quotes: RebLaw is the nation's largest student-run public interest conference.

The conference seeks to build a community of law students, practitioners, and activists seeking to work in the service of social change movements and to challenge hierarchies of race, wealth, gender, and expertise within legal practice and education.
key_insight: Legal activism and social movements like Occupy Wall Street can intersect to challenge systemic inequalities in finance and governance.
